title: Specklinia spathuliglossa
taxonomy: Specklinia spathuliglossa is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Specklinia, which is a small genus of orchids commonly found in Central and South America. Specklinia spathuliglossa is classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on the ground rather than in trees. The species is further classified as a type of pleuroterpoid orchid due to its unique leaf structure. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary history and relationships between different Specklinia species.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections.
